human body: The human body has 37.2 trillion cells, 206 bones, 600+ muscles, and 60,000 miles of blood vessels. Organized into 11 organ systems.
skeletal system: 206 bones providing structure, protecting organs, producing blood cells, and storing minerals. Femur is largest bone, stapes is smallest.
muscular system: 600+ muscles. Three types: skeletal (voluntary), smooth (involuntary), cardiac (heart). Make up ~40% of body weight.
circulatory system: Heart pumps 5 litres/min through 60,000 miles of vessels. Arteries carry oxygenated blood away, veins return deoxygenated blood.
nervous system: 86 billion neurons in the brain, 100 trillion synaptic connections. Central nervous system (brain + spinal cord) and peripheral nervous system.
respiratory system: Lungs with 300-500 million alveoli. Surface area of ~100 m². Adult breathes 12-20 times per minute at rest.
digestive system: Small intestine is 20 feet long. Stomach produces HCl and digestive enzymes. Liver performs 500+ functions. Process takes 24-72 hours.
reproductive system: Male: testes, vas deferens, prostate, penis. Female: ovaries, fallopian tubes, uterus, cervix, vagina. Gestation ~40 weeks.
the brain: 1.4 kg organ, 73% water, consumes 20% of body's oxygen. Four lobes: frontal, parietal, temporal, occipital. 86 billion neurons.
the heart: Fist-sized muscular organ. Beats 100,000 times/day, pumps 7,600 litres/day. Four chambers. SA node is natural pacemaker.
the lungs: Right lung has 3 lobes, left has 2. Total capacity 4-6 litres. 20,000 breaths/day. Alveoli surface area equals a tennis court.
dna: Double helix with 3 billion base pairs per cell nucleus. 99.9% identical between any two people. 20,000-25,000 genes in human genome.
